title.en: I'm sorry, it's my mistake! (Apology and Handling)

description.en: When you're late for an appointment or make a small mistake! Practice reacting with a sincere but natural apology like 미안해요 and 제 실수예요.
description.ko: 약속에 늦었거나 작은 실수를 했을 때! 너무 무겁지 않으면서도 진심이 느껴지게 사과하고 대화를 이어가는 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Daily Life. Apology. Mistake. Friendship. Social. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.emotion: 미안해서 어쩔 줄 모르는 목소리로 바로 시작
content.text: Mina I'm so sorry! I just realized I forgot to bring the book you lent me. I left it right on my desk!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아 진짜요? 오늘 같이 읽으려고 했는데 아쉽네요!
content.translation: Ah, really? I was hoping to read it together today, so that's a shame!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이럴 때 당황해서 '어떡해' 라고 반복하기보다는 깔끔하게 사과하고 상황을 정리하는 리액션이 중요해요.
content.translation: In times like this, rather than just repeating "What should I do!" in a panic, a reaction that clearly apologizes and settles the situation is important.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Right. I just kept saying "미안해요" over and over. Was that enough?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 음..., 사과 뒤에 '내 실수다' 라고 인정하는 말을 덧붙이면 훨씬 진심이 느껴져요.
content.translation: Well, if you add words admitting "it's my mistake" after the apology, it feels much more sincere.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 제 실수예요. This is the natural version of the formal one we talked about before, right?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! '실수예요' 를 써서 훨씬 부드러워졌죠? 친구나 가까운 사이에서 내 잘못을 인정할 때 딱 좋아요.
content.translation: Exactly! By using -예요 instead of -습니다, it became much softer, right? It's perfect for admitting your fault with friends or someone close.

insight.title: 제 실수예요
insight.usage_tip: '제(My)' + '실수(Mistake)' + '예요(Is)'. Using '제' instead of '내' adds a touch of politeness even in casual settings.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 미안해요. 제 실수예요.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Then what about 다음부터는 안 그럴게요?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이건 "앞으로 조심하겠다" 라는 약속이에요. 상대방의 화를 누그러뜨리는 마법 같은 리액션이죠.
content.translation: This is a promise that "I will be careful in the future." It's a magic reaction that softens the other person's anger.

insight.title: 다음부터는 안 그럴게요
insight.usage_tip: '다음(Next)' + '부터(From)' + '안(Not)' + '그럴게요(Will do so)'. A friendly way to promise improvement.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 정말 미안해요. 다음부터는 안 그럴게요!
content.translation: I'm really sorry. I won't do that next time!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. We were supposed to meet at a cafe at 2 PM, but I'm 15 minutes late.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 어? 존! 왜 이제 와요? 십오분이나 기다렸잖아요!
content.translation: Oh, John! Why are you just arriving now? I've been waiting for 15 minutes!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 많이 기다렸죠? 정말 미안해요. 제 실수예요.
content.translation: you waited a long time, right? I'm truly sorry. It's my mistake.

insight.title: 정말 미안해요
insight.usage_tip: Adding '정말' or '진짜' makes the apology feel more sincere in everyday conversation.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘 날씨도 더운데 연락이라도 미리 해주지 그랬어요!
content.translation: The weather is hot today too... you should have at least contacted me in advance!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 오는 길에 휴대폰 배터리가 나가서... 다음부터는 절대 안 그럴게요. 제가 시원한 커피 살게요!
content.translation: My phone battery died on the way... I definitely won't do that next time. I'll buy you a cool coffee!

insight.title: 절대 안 그럴게요
insight.usage_tip: Adding '절대(Never)' emphasizes your strong intention to be better.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오? 커피요? 그럼 용서해 줄게요. 빨리 주문하러 가요!
content.translation: Oh, coffee? Then I'll forgive you. Let's go order quickly!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Phew, a sincere apology plus a coffee really fixed the mood!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 실수를 했을 땐 변명보다 먼저 미안해요 라고 말하는 리액션이 가장 중요해요.
content.translation: Haha, right. When you make a mistake, the most important reaction is saying "I'm sorry" first rather than making excuses.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "daily apology" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 미안해요. 제 실수예요! I'm sorry, it's my mistake!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 다음부터는 안 그럴게요! I won't do that next time!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 깜빡했어요. 미안해요! I forgot, I'm sorry!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 많이 기다렸죠? 죄송해요! Did you wait long? I'm sorry!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 제가 더 신경 쓸게요! I'll pay more attention!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 작은 실수를 했다면 숨기지 말고 웃으면서 먼저 사과해 보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, if you made a small mistake today, don't hide it and try apologizing first with a smile. "I'm sorry, it's my mistake!"

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 누구나 실수를 할 수 있지만 사과하는 게 중요해요. (Anyone can make a mistake, but it's important to apologize.)
약속 시간을 깜빡해서 정말 미안해요. (I'm so sorry I suddenly forgot the appointment time.)
그의 진심 어린 사과에 화가 누그러졌어요. (My anger softened at his sincere apology.)
친구가 사과를 하길래 바로 용서해 줬어요. (Since my friend apologized, I forgave them right away.)
잘못을 했을 때는 변명보다 인정을 먼저 하세요. (When you've done something wrong, admit it first rather than making an excuse.)
다음에는 더 신경 써서 준비할게요. (I'll prepare with more attention next time.)
